steps.title: Identify the relationship between the two sentences
steps.content:
The first sentence says the soldiers’ contribution was overlooked for decades. The second sentence says that in 2017, lawmakers declared recognition. These two events are sequential in time — long neglect followed by eventual honor. The transition word must bridge a time gap, not compare or list.
  • Choice A — 'Lastly,': “Lastly” signals the final item in an enumerated list. There is no list here. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Then,': “Then” marks what happened next in time — after decades of being overlooked, then in 2017 recognition came. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Similarly,': “Similarly” signals that the second sentence resembles the first. Being overlooked and receiving recognition are opposites, not similar. ✗
  • Choice D — 'For example,': “For example” introduces an illustration of the previous point. Recognition in 2017 is not an example of being overlooked. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content:
“Then” is the only transition that captures the temporal sequence: decades of neglect followed by eventual acknowledgment in 2017.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
